channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple launches Invites, its event invitation app that takes on Partiful
item.description: Apple rolled out its newest iPhone app called Invites, which lets iCloud+ subscribers create and share invitations to anyone, regardless of whether they have an Apple account or Apple device. Hosts have control over the events.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Google's AI Bet Raises Concerns Amid Earnings Growth
item.description: Alphabet Inc.'s Q4 earnings show growth, but AI expansion costs and regulatory concerns weigh on investors' minds.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Another mini PC with a Ryzen AI 9 HX CPU has emerged, and not only does it support four 4K monitors, it can also take up to 96GB memory
item.description: Acemagic F3A mini PC combines cutting-edge AMD Ryzen AI technology with impressive performance capabilities for demanding users.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly available pornography app available in the European Union, and warned that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7: latest news, rumors, and everything we’ve heard so far
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 could land in July, with a bigger screen and more power than the current model.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Three ways VMware customers can buy time and extend value
item.description: As the IT landscape continually evolves, CIOs and IT decision makers face an increasingly complex array of challenges. The most pressing involve decisions concerning when to adopt new technologies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: China to investigate Google amid tensions with US
item.description: China said Tuesday it is investigating Google for potential antitrust violations after the Trump administration levied new tariffs on Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: DHS research in airport passenger screening never stops
item.description: "We use millimeter technology to collect images of passengers to ensure that no threats that are not allowed on the plane," said John Fortune The post DHS research in airport passenger screening never stops first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta sets Oculus Quest headset shelf-life at six years, but there's still hope that the Meta Quest 2 will survive past 2026
item.description: Meta has ended all software updates to the Oculus Quest, which suggests the Quest 2 could enter stasis in 2026.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: NASA Tests Moon Gravity on Blue Origin Flight - The News
item.description: Jeff Bezos' rocket company, Blue Origin, launches NASA experiments to test lunar gravity at the edge of space from West Texas, achieving artificial moon gravity for a brief period.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New EU Chat Control makes scanning encrypted chats optional – but privacy experts are still worried
item.description: Under Poland's proposal, the scanning becomes optional. Yet, privacy experts are still warning against unattended consequences.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: How an ancient asteroid strike carved out 2 grand canyons on the moon
item.description: New research shows that when an asteroid slammed into the moon billions of years ago, it carved out a pair of grand canyons on the far side.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: D-Marin transforma su escenario digital con Microsoft Dynamics 365
item.description: La compañía especializada en la gestión de puertos deportivos, D-Marin, se apoya en el experto en Microsoft, Avanade, para el despliegue e implantación de Microsoft Dynamics 365.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Linux Foundation warns of US OFAC sanctions
item.description: Developers must be cautious about who they interact with and where contributions come from, warns a Linux Foundation blog post intended to help open-source developers navigate US OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control) sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Musk adds Shell, Nestlé, Lego to X advertiser 'boycott' lawsuit
item.description: Elon Musk’s X is adding several more companies to its lawsuit accusing firms of conspiring in an advertising "boycott" on the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Santa Clara County accuses companies of causing ‘environmental and human health crisis’ via ‘forever chemicals’ in drinking water
item.description: The legal action comes amid worldwide attention on PFAS, used widely to create products resistant to water, stains, and heat, including food packaging, non-stick pans, clothing, rain jackets, furniture, carpeting, plastic turf, paints, electronics products and a host of other goods.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This free music-streaming site can replace your Spotify subscription
item.description: You know what I miss? Listening to the radio. I’ve always loved background music, which helps me focus. But modern music-streaming services can be distracting. Yes, I enjoy having instant access to millions of songs with services like Spotify.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI unleashes o3-mini reasoning model
item.description: OpenAI on Friday released the latest model in its reasoning series, o3-mini, both in ChatGPT and its application programming interface (API). It had been in preview since December 2024.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The quantum computing reality check
item.description: In late 2024, a major pharmaceutical company invested $50 million in quantum computing initiatives through a leading cloud provider, hoping to revolutionize their drug discovery process.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Timeline of HPE’s $14 billion bid for Juniper
item.description: AI is a key factor in one of the tech industry’s most significant M&A developments: HPE’s proposed acquisition of Juniper Networks. The $14 billion deal would shake up the industry, and regulatory watchdogs in the U.S. and worldwide have raised antitrust concerns.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim
item.description: "Data centers is one of the new industrial sectors that we've been looking at because it's part of the emerging economy and new technologies," said Arman Shehab The post Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Industry Exchange Cyber 2025: Zimperium’s Kern Smith on why securing mobile devices doesn’t necessarily mean locking them down
item.description: With the right continuous monitoring of mobile activity and apps, agencies can avoid restrictive device policies, advises Zimperium’s solutions engineering VP.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft’s new DocumentDB rethinks NoSQL on PostgreSQL
item.description: Microsoft’s recent launch of a standalone version of the MongoDB compatibility layer for its global-scale Azure Cosmos DB brought back an old name. Back in 2018, when the company unveiled a public version of the Project Florence database engine that powers much of Azure, they called it DocumentDB.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How to make sure you’ve got the cybersecurity people you need
item.description: The government's personnel shop has devised a five-part strategy for planning, hiring and retaining cybersecurity people. But not everyone follows the strategy. The post How to make sure you’ve got the cybersecurity people you need first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: DeepSeek underscores US-China race for AI dominance, OpenAI says
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
